steps: From the table, read paired (food calories, kilojoules) values per gram. Fat: 9 kcal, 37.7 kJ. Carbs: 4 kcal, 16.7 kJ. Protein: 4 kcal, 16.7 kJ.
Compute kJ per food calorie: 37.7 / 9 ≈ 4.19, 16.7 / 4 ≈ 4.18. The ratio is consistently ≈ 4.2.
choiceElimination:
• Choice A — 'k = 0.24x': 0.24 ≈ 1/4.2, which is the inverse ratio (calories per kJ, not kJ per calorie). ✗
  • Choice B — 'k = 4.2x': k (kJ) = 4.2 × x (food calories) matches the table ratio of ~4.2 kJ per food calorie. ✓
  • Choice C — 'x = 4.2k': This says food calories = 4.2 × kJ, which inverts the relationship. ✗
  • Choice D — 'xk = 4.2': This makes x and k inversely proportional (product = 4.2), but they are directly proportional. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
